verdict_reasoning: HSPA5 (GRP78/BiP) is canonically an ER-lumenal chaperone with a KDEL retention motif. Under ER stress, hypoxia, nutrient deprivation, oncogenic transformation, and therapeutic resistance, a subfraction escapes KDEL-mediated retrieval and translocates to the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane (csGRP78/sGRP78). This cancer/stress-restricted surface pool is confirmed by flow cytometry, surface biotinylation, and surface proteomics across breast, prostate, glioma, leukemia, and other tumor types. Multiple antibody and targeted-therapy programs explicitly engage csGRP78 on the extracellular face of live tumor cells. Surface display is absent or minimal in unstressed normal cells, making this a textbook cell_state_induced ecto-chaperone.
